title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description: The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that enables large-scale data processing tasks. It consists of a cluster of nodes, each running a task tracker, and a centralized job tracker that coordinates the tasks. The architecture supports various types of tasks, including map, reduce, and shuffle, and provides fault tolerance and scalability. The MapReduce architecture is highly scalable, fault-tolerant, and provides performance improvements through data locality and speculative execution. It uses the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) for distributed storage and communication between nodes.
raw_response:
TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture

DESCRIPTION:
The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that enables large-scale data processing tasks. It consists of a cluster of nodes, each running a task tracker, and a centralized job tracker that coordinates the tasks. The architecture supports various types of tasks, including map, reduce, and shuffle, and provides fault tolerance and scalability. The MapReduce architecture is highly scalable, fault-tolerant, and provides performance improvements through data locality and speculative execution. It uses the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) for distributed storage and communication between nodes.
